title_en: Introduction to Al-Mughni and Hanbali Jurisprudence
content_en:
Al-Mughni (The Sufficient One) stands as one of the most monumental achievements in the history of Islamic jurisprudence. Written by Muwaffaq ad-Din Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi (541–620 AH / 1147–1223 CE), this encyclopedic work spans fifteen large volumes and covers virtually every topic of Islamic law with meticulous scholarly care. It is rightly regarded as the most comprehensive Hanbali fiqh encyclopedia ever written and one of the greatest works of Islamic legal literature from any school.

Ibn Qudamah was born in the village of Jammain near Nablus in Palestine (present-day West Bank). When the Crusader occupation made life difficult for Muslims in the region, his family emigrated to Damascus, where he grew up and pursued his education. He later traveled to Baghdad, the center of Hanbali scholarship, where he studied for four years under the leading scholars of the school before returning to Damascus, where he taught and wrote until his death.

The Hanbali school — one of the four accepted Sunni madhabs — was founded by Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (164–241 AH / 780–855 CE), the great hadith scholar and theologian who is perhaps best known for his steadfast refusal to affirm the Mutazilite position on the created nature of the Quran during the Mihna (Inquisition) under the Abbasid caliphs. Imam Ahmad's approach to fiqh was distinguished by a very strong preference for authentic hadith over analogical reasoning. He was reluctant to issue opinions where hadith was absent and cautious about extending legal principles through qiyas (analogy) in ways not clearly sanctioned by the prophetic tradition.

Al-Mughni was written as an expansion of the Mukhtasar (abridgment) of al-Khiraqi, an earlier Hanbali jurist. For each issue al-Khiraqi raised, Ibn Qudamah presents the Hanbali position in full, then surveys the positions of the other Sunni schools (and sometimes the Shia and other groups), citing their evidence, analyzing the strength of each argument, and explaining why the Hanbali position is preferred — or, in some cases, acknowledging that another position is equally or more well-supported by the evidence.

This comparative method makes Al-Mughni far more than a Hanbali manual. It is a work of serious comparative jurisprudence (fiqh muqaran) that any scholar of Islamic law — regardless of their own school — must engage with. Ibn Rushd's Bidayat al-Mujtahid and Al-Mughni stand as the two pinnacles of classical comparative fiqh, approaching the subject from different angles but with equal rigor.

Ibn Qudamah's scholarship was matched by his personal character. He was known for deep piety, generosity, and a commitment to the prophetic way that extended beyond mere legal expertise into genuine spiritual life. His other major works include Al-Kafi (a more concise Hanbali manual), Rawdat an-Nadhir (a work on legal theory, usul al-fiqh), and Al-Muqni' (a middle-length legal manual that became the basis for Al-Mughni).

For scholars, students, and serious readers, Al-Mughni remains an indispensable reference — a window into the breadth and depth of classical Islamic legal thought at its most authoritative.
title_id: Pengantar Al-Mughni dan Fikih Mazhab Hanbali
content_id:
Al-Mughni (Yang Mencukupi) merupakan salah satu pencapaian paling monumental dalam sejarah fikih Islam. Ditulis oleh Muwaffaq ad-Din Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi (541-620 H / 1147-1223 M), kitab ini terdiri dari beberapa jilid besar yang membahas seluruh cabang fikih mazhab Hanbali secara mendalam dan sistematis. Karya ini tidak hanya menyajikan pendapat mazhab Hanbali, tetapi juga membandingkannya dengan mazhab-mazhab lain dengan metodologi ilmiah yang ketat.

Ibn Qudamah menyusun Al-Mughni sebagai syarah (komentar) atas Mukhtasar al-Khiraqi, salah satu matan fikih Hanbali yang terkenal. Keistimewaan Al-Mughni terletak pada kedalaman penguasaan hadis, kemampuan beliau dalam membandingkan pendapat para imam mazhab, serta ketajaman dalam menilai dalil-dalil setiap pendapat. Para ulama menjadikannya referensi utama dalam fikih Hanbali hingga hari ini.
